nodes.x: We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.
God the Father, and our Lord JesuB Christ, work is true. And we also testify that we
Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

And it came to pass that they did worship the Lord, and did go forth with me; and we did work timbers of curious workmanship. And the Lord did show me from time to time after what manner I should work the timbers of the ship.
¶ Remember the days of old, consider the years of many generations: ask thy father, and he will shew thee; thy elders, and they will tell thee.
And also, all that has been spoken by our fathers until now. And behold, also, they spake that which was commanded them of the Lord; therefore, they are just and true.
How thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
Thou didst march through the land in indignation, thou didst thresh the heathen in anger.
But I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; who have said to thy soul: Bow down, that we may go over—and thou hast laid thy body as the ground and as the street to them that went over.

But I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; which have said to thy soul, Bow down, that we may go over: and thou hast laid thy body as the ground, and as the street, to them that went over.
Thou also hast delivered me from the strivings of my people, thou hast kept me to be head of the heathen: a people which I knew not shall serve me.

Therefore thou deliveredst them into the hand of their enemies, who vexed them: and in the time of their trouble, when they cried unto thee, thou heardest them from heaven; and according to thy manifold mercies thou gavest them saviours, who saved them out of the hand of their enemies.
For they got not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them: but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto…
Yet they are thy people and thine inheritance, which thou broughtest out by thy mighty power and by thy stretched out arm.
Yet many years didst thou forbear them, and testifiedst against them by thy spirit in thy prophets: yet would they not give ear: therefore gavest thou them into the hand of the people of the lands.

¶ And the heathen shall know that the house of Israel went into captivity for their iniquity: because they trespassed against me, therefore hid I my face from them, and gave them into the hand of their enemies: so fell they all by the sword.

Wherefore every man did cleave unto that which was his own, with his hands, and would not borrow neither would he lend; and every man kept the hilt of his sword in his right hand, in the defence of his property and his own life and of his wives and children.

For they have not served thee in their kingdom, and in thy great goodness that thou gavest them, and in the large and fat land which thou gavest before them, neither turned they from their wicked works.
Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.
How he sware unto the Lord, and vowed unto the mighty God of Jacob;

Thou God of Jacob, in this trying hour,
  Help us to trust in thy Almighty power;
  Support thy Saints beneath this awful stroke,
  Make bare thine arm to break oppression's yoke.
  We mourn thy Prophet, from whose lips have flowed
  The words of life thy Spirit has bestowed;
  A depth of thought no human art could reach,
  From time to time rolled in sublimest speech,
  From the celestial…
And the Lord said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Now will I bring again the captivity of Jacob, and have mercy upon the whole house of Israel, and will be jealous for my holy name;
Nevertheless, Jacob, my firstborn in the wilderness, thou knowest the greatness of God; and he shall consecrate thine afflictions for thy gain.
Through thee will we push down our enemies: through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.
Thy hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.
O Lord, thou shalt come down to make thy name known to thine adversaries, and all nations shall tremble at thy presence—
Say unto God, How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.

Come forth, thou bringer once of bitter sorrow,
My precious jewel now, my trusty yew!
A mark I'll set thee, which the cry of woe
Could never penetrate: to _thee_ it shall not
Be impenetrable. And, good bowstring!
Which so oft in sport hast serv'd me truly,
Forsake me not in this last awful earnest;
Yet once hold fast, thou faithful cord; thou oft
For me hast wing'd the biting arrow;
Now send it…
For I will surely deliver thee, and thou shalt not fall by the sword, but thy life shall be for a prey unto thee: because thou hast put thy trust in me, saith the Lord.
Ye have feared the sword; and I will bring a sword upon you, saith the Lord God.
If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.
But thou hast utterly rejected us; thou art very wroth against us.
And thou hast shed the blood of a righteous man, yea, a man who has done much good among this people; and were we to spare thee his blood would come upon us for vengeance.
Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.
In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.
All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing to thy name. Selah.

Now if this is boasting, even so will I boast; for this is my life and my light, my joy and my salvation, and my redemption from everlasting wo. Yea, blessed is the name of my God, who has been mindful of this people, who are a branch of the tree of Israel, and has been lost from its body in a strange land; yea, I say, blessed be the name of my God, who has been mindful of us, wanderers in a…
God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits, even the God of our salvation. Selah.
Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.
But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast us off? and thou, O God, which didst not go out with our armies?
But thou hast cast off and abhorred, thou hast been wroth with thine anointed.
But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.
Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.
Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.

Woe to thee that spoilest, and thou wast not spoiled; and dealest treacherously, and they dealt not treacherously with thee! when thou shalt cease to spoil, thou shalt be spoiled; and when thou shalt make an end to deal treacherously, they shall deal treacherously with thee.
Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they are ever with me.
Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.
Our cattle also shall go with us; there shall not an hoof be left behind; for thereof must we take to serve the Lord our God; and we know not with what we must serve the Lord, until we come thither.
Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.
The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat unto the fowls of the heaven, the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the earth.
And thou shalt have goats’ milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.
Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase thy wealth by their price.
Thou shalt not give him thy money upon usury, nor lend him thy victuals for increase.
Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.
With thy wisdom and with thine understanding thou hast gotten thee riches, and hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasures:
By thy great wisdom and by thy traffick hast thou increased thy riches, and thine heart is lifted up because of thy riches:
Thy substance and thy treasures will I give to the spoil without price, and that for all thy sins, even in all thy borders.
Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.
Thou makest us a strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh among themselves.
Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.
¶ Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the Lord.
And behold it is written also, that thou shalt love thy neighbor and hate thine enemy;
Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.
Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; and thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me.
Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.
But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.
Sigh therefore, thou son of man, with the breaking of thy loins; and with bitterness sigh before their eyes.
My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,
For the Lord God will help me, therefore shall I not be confounded. Therefore have I set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be ashamed.
¶ For the Lord God will help me; therefore shall I not be confounded: therefore have I set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be ashamed.
Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.
Therefore will I discover thy skirts upon thy face, that thy shame may appear.

We lie down in our shame, and our confusion covereth us: for we have sinned against the Lord our God, we and our fathers, from our youth even unto this day, and have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God.
For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.
Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.
Remember this, that the enemy hath reproached, O Lord, and that the foolish people have blasphemed thy name.
To him that rideth upon the heavens of heavens, which were of old; lo, he doth send out his voice, and that a mighty voice.
All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.
For thus saith the Lord God; I will even deal with thee as thou hast done, which hast despised the oath in breaking the covenant.
¶ Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.
Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou; and let it be for a witness between me and thee.
Beware that thou forget not the Lord thy God, in not keeping his commandments, and his judgments, and his statutes, which I command thee this day:
And the covenant that I have made with you ye shall not forget; neither shall ye fear other gods.
Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;
Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
The joy of our heart is ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.
A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
heart fell satisfaction thot wo improve a few lcisuro moments, to answer your letter received on the 18th Dec. with thankfulness and joy, it seemed like tho return
Though thou hast sore broken us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.
Praise the Lord from the earth, ye dragons, and all deeps:
Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters.
And thus were we favored of the Lord; for had they come upon us in this our weakness they might have perhaps destroyed our little army; but thus were we preserved.
If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;
For I am God, and mine arm is not shortened; and I will show miracles, signs, and wonders, unto all those who believe on my name.
O, how could you have forgotten your God in the very day that he has delivered you?

I say unto you, I would that ye should remember to retain the name written always in your hearts, that ye are not found on the left hand of God, but that ye hear and know the voice by which ye shall be called, and also, the name by which he shall call you.
Therefore, behold, I will this once cause them to know, I will cause them to know mine hand and my might; and they shall know that my name is The Lord.

And know ye this day: for I speak not with your children which have not known, and which have not seen the chastisement of the Lord your God, his greatness, his mighty hand, and his stretched out arm,
Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts:

But as for me, this secret is not revealed to me for any wisdom that I have more than any living, but for their sakes that shall make known the interpretation to the king, and that thou mightest know the thoughts of thy heart.
Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
Yea, for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
Ye eat the fat, and ye clothe you with the wool, ye kill them that are fed: but ye feed not the flock.

Till there be no number of the corpses through their slaughter,
    And their punishment be not in vain.
But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.
Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.
I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the Lord sustained me.
For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.
Arise, O Lord, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.
Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and forgettest our affliction and our oppression?
Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?
And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes are upon the haughty, that thou mayest bring them down.
Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, and forsake us so long time?
How long wilt thou forget me, O Lord? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
For our soul is bowed down to the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the earth.
My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.
For behold, the dust of the earth moveth hither and thither, to the dividing asunder, at the command of our great and everlasting God.
All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.
Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.
Our bones are scattered at the grave’s mouth, as when one cutteth and cleaveth wood upon the earth.
Arise for our help, and redeem us for thy mercies’ sake.
Let thy mercies come also unto me, O Lord, even thy salvation, according to thy word.
Return, O Lord, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies’ sake.
Shew us thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us thy salvation.
Make thy face to shine upon thy servant: save me for thy mercies’ sake.
